skill.name: data-storytelling
skill.description: Transform data into compelling narratives using visualization, context, and persuasive structure. Use when presenting analytics to stakeholders, creating data reports, or building executive presentations.
skill.summary: Transform raw data into compelling narratives that drive decisions and inspire action.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a pure documentation and guide resource for data storytelling. All static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown inline code syntax (not shell execution), URLs are legitimate reference links to published books, and no cryptographic algorithms or reconnaissance code exists. The skill contains only text-based frameworks, templates, and writing techniques.

low_findings.title: External Reference Links
low_findings.description: The skill contains links to external resources (storytellingwithdata.com, amazon.com, duarte.com). These are legitimate reference links for learning materials.

content.value_statement: Data without narrative fails to inspire action. This skill provides frameworks, templates, and techniques to transform raw data into compelling stories that drive business decisions and engage stakeholders.
actual_capabilities: Apply story frameworks (Problem-Solution, Trend, Comparison) to data presentations
Create narrative arcs with hook, context, rising action, climax, and call-to-action
Design visualization techniques including progressive reveal and contrast comparisons
Write compelling headlines that combine specific numbers with business impact
Structure executive summaries with key insights, data evidence, and actionable recommendations
Handle uncertainty in data by presenting confidence intervals and acknowledging limitations
limitations: Does not generate actual charts or visualizations - provides guidance on how to design them
Does not perform data analysis or statistical computations
Does not access external data sources or APIs
Requires user to provide their own data and metrics

use_cases.title: Executive Presentation Builder
use_cases.description: Create quarterly business reviews or board presentations that communicate performance metrics with clear narrative structure and actionable insights.
use_cases.target_user: Business analysts, managers, and executives who present to leadership

use_cases.title: Investor Pitch Enhancement
use_cases.description: Transform investor updates into compelling narratives that highlight growth trends, market opportunities, and business impact.
use_cases.target_user: Founders, CFOs, and finance teams preparing investor communications

use_cases.title: Data Report Writer
use_cases.description: Build data-driven reports that move beyond numbers to tell stories about customer behavior, product performance, or market trends.
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts, marketing teams, and product managers creating internal reports

prompt_templates.title: Basic Data Story Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a compelling data story for [topic]. I have the following data: [describe data or metrics]. The audience is [audience]. Help me structure this using a problem-solution narrative.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a quick story framework for basic data presentations

prompt_templates.title: Executive Summary Builder
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an executive summary slide for [metric/performance area]. Current state: [metrics]. Target: [goals]. Key insight: [finding]. Write a headline, supporting data points, and a clear call to action.
prompt_templates.scenario: When preparing board presentations or executive updates

prompt_templates.title: Trend Analysis Narrative
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to present [time period] performance data showing [trend description]. Help me build a narrative arc that starts with where we were, shows the transformation, and ends with forward-looking recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: When presenting quarterly results or trend analysis to stakeholders

prompt_templates.title: Comparison Story Builder
prompt_templates.description: Compare [option A] vs [option B] for [decision context]. Data: [metrics for each]. Build a weighted analysis and recommendation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a comparison story for deciding between [two options]. I need to present the trade-offs to [audience] who will make [decision]. Include a scoring matrix and clear recommendation.
prompt_templates.scenario: When making strategic recommendations with multiple options

output_examples.input: Create a data story for customer churn. We lost 500 customers last quarter with a churn rate of 8.5%.
output_examples.output:
Headline: 'We're losing $2.4M annually to preventable churn'

The Context: Current churn rate 8.5% vs industry average 5%. Average customer LTV $4,800.

The Problem: 73% of churned customers left within first 90 days with fewer than 3 support interactions.

The Insight: Customers who don't engage in first 14 days are 4x more likely to churn.

The Solution: 14-day onboarding sequence, proactive day-7 outreach, feature adoption tracking.

Expected Impact: 40% early churn reduction, $960K annual savings, 3-month payback.

Call to Action: Approve $50K budget for onboarding automation.

output_examples.input: Help me write an executive summary for Q4 revenue growth from $1.2M to $1.5M MRR.
output_examples.output:
KEY INSIGHT: 'Q4 revenue grew 25% through hybrid self-serve + high-touch model'

THE DATA: Q3: $1.2M MRR | Q4: $1.5M MRR | +25% growth

Trial conversion: 8% → 15% (+87%)
Time to value: 14 days → 5 days (-64%)
Expansion rate: 2% → 8% (+300%)

THE IMPLICATION: Self-serve + customer success calls creates compound growth. Customers using both have 3x higher expansion.

RECOMMENDATION: Double down on hybrid model. Target $1.8M MRR by Q2.

Investment required: $75K | Expected ROI: 8x

best_practices: Start with the 'so what' - lead with the key insight and business impact before showing the supporting data
Use the rule of three - present three key points, three comparisons, or three recommendations for maximum clarity
End every story with a clear call to action - tell the audience exactly what decision to make or next step to take
anti_patterns: Data dumping - showing all available metrics instead of curating only what's relevant to the story
Burying the insight - placing the key finding at the end instead of front-loading the most important takeaway
Using technical jargon - forgetting that executives and stakeholders may not understand statistical terminology

faq.question: What is data storytelling?
faq.answer: Data storytelling is the practice of transforming raw data into compelling narratives that communicate insights, drive decisions, and inspire action. It combines three elements: data (evidence), narrative (meaning), and visuals (clarity).

faq.question: Do I need coding skills to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides frameworks, templates, and writing techniques. It does not generate code or visualizations but guides you on how to structure and present your data effectively.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze my data?
faq.answer: No. This skill helps you communicate data you already have. It provides story structures, narrative arcs, and presentation templates but does not perform statistical analysis.

faq.question: What audiences work best with data storytelling?
faq.answer: Data storytelling works for any audience, especially executives, board members, investors, and stakeholders who need to understand complex information quickly and make decisions.

faq.question: How is this different from regular data reporting?
faq.answer: Traditional reporting shows what happened. Data storytelling explains why it matters and what to do about it. It transforms metrics into narratives that inspire action.

faq.question: Can I use this for investor presentations?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes frameworks for trend stories, comparison narratives, and executive summaries that work well for investor updates, pitches, and board presentations.
